title: Book
text:
Time Series Econometrics: Learning Through Replication
Springer Texts in Business and Economics
Second Edition, 2023 | First Edition, 2018
Springer International Publishing | 488 pages




Springer | Amazon
This textbook teaches time series econometrics through replication rather than theorem and proof. Students work through landmark papers in economics and finance — by Granger and Newbold, Nelson and Plosser, Perron, Bollerslev, Sims, Arellano and Bond, and others — using the original data and reproducing published results in Stata. Topics progress from univariate ARMA models through unit root testing, structural breaks, ARCH/GARCH models, vector autoregressions, and static and dynamic panel data models. The book includes many worked examples and data-driven exercises, and is intended for graduate students, advanced undergraduates, and practitioners.

title: Book

title: John Levendis
text:
I am an Economist working as Professor of Practice in Data Science at the Connolly Alexander Institute for Data Science (CAIDS) at Tulane University.
My research focuses on the effect of technology on the economy. Specifically, I study how IT infrastructure affects firm performance and financial markets in developing countries.
I teach courses in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and statistics.
I also write about research and data science at Substack.

title: Research
text:
My research examines IT infrastructure, market efficiency, and firm performance, with a particular focus on emerging economies. I use econometric methods including panel data models, VAR, and unit root tests.
For a complete list of publications, see my Google Scholar profile or ORCID profile.
